dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8557 IH: Short-Term Holding Facility Standards Restoration Act.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Stanton (for himself, Ms. Ansari, and Mrs. Grijalva) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To prohibit long-term custody in U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement holding facilities, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Short-Term Holding Facility Standards Restoration Act.

paragraph.text: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement holding facilities are designed for short-term custody and processing.
Prior U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement policy defined short-term as not exceeding 12 hours, absent exceptional circumstances.
Prolonged detention in holding facilities undermines detainee welfare, facility design limits, and operational intent.
Restoring the 12-hour standard ensures consistency with prior agency guidance.

subsection.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall ensure that, absent exceptional circumstances described in subsection (b), a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement holding facility may only be used for short-term custody of a detainee.
Exceptional circumstances are temporary and unforeseen circumstances requiring immediate action, including—

paragraph.text: transportation or transfer delays beyond the control of the Department of Homeland Security; or
other exigent operational conditions as determined by the Secretary of Homeland Security.

section.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall ensure that—

paragraph.text: each U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement holding facility is safe, clean, equipped with restroom facilities, and clear of any object that could be used as a weapon; and

subparagraph.text: is provided a meal not less than every 6 hours;
in the case of a minor, pregnant woman, or breastfeeding woman, is provided immediate access to meals, snacks, milk, and juice, without regard to the amount of time in custody; and
is provided with access to drinking water in each room in which a detainee is held at all times.

subsection.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall require contemporaneous documentation of any instance in which a detainee is held in a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement holding facility for custody other than short-term custody.
Records under this section shall be maintained for oversight, audit, and reporting purposes for a period of 5 years.
The Secretary of Homeland Security shall submit to Congress an annual report detailing—

paragraph.text: the number of detainees held in custody other than short-term custody in a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement holding facility;
the justification for each instance in which a detainee is held in a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement holding facility for custody other than short-term custody; and
corrective actions taken to ensure compliance.

subsection.text: The Inspector General of the Department of Homeland Security shall perform periodic audits of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement holding facilities and Department of Homeland Security records to ensure compliance with this Act.

section.text: For the purposes of this Act—

paragraph.text: the term holding facility means any facility or holding room for temporary custody, processing, or transfer, and not designed for long-term detention; and
the term short-term custody means detention in a holding facility for a period not to exceed 12 hours, absent exceptional circumstances.
